title: [lichen_l2.c:339-378] peer_try_all_pubkeys O(n) search timing side-channel
description: The peer_try_all_pubkeys() function iterates through all registered peers trying signature verification until one succeeds. This creates a timing side-channel: an attacker can distinguish between 'no peers registered' (fast return), 'peer 1 matched' (1 verify attempt), 'peer N matched' (N verify attempts). While the comment acknowledges the O(n) complexity (line 315), it doesn't address that this leaks information about peer table structure and which peer authenticated a given frame. For mesh networks with sensitive peer relationships, this could be exploited. Consider constant-time peer enumeration or rate limiting responses.
close_reason: Fixed: peer_try_all_pubkeys now uses constant-time iteration - always checks all peers even after finding a match, storing found_idx and returning after full loop
